title: IntentLock: A Deterministic Intent-to-Commit Control and Evidence Architecture for AI-Assisted Software Development
description: IntentLock is a proposed local-first control and evidence architecture for binding a signed human-approved software task to the repository state ultimately accepted after an AI coding session. It converts human authority into deterministic policy and capability requirements, runs changes in a non-authoritative workspace, verifies frozen candidates against Diff Envelopes and security gates, admits changes through an independent acceptance boundary, and produces an offline intent-to-commit proof capsule. The proposed contribution is the integrated authority and evidence chain, not the novelty of sandboxing, policy evaluation, Git review, or attestations individually. This independent technical blueprint does not claim implementation, validation, patentability, legal compliance, universal security, peer review, or production readiness.
notes: Independent Research Publication No. 3. v5.0 Public Research Edition. Not peer reviewed. Proposed architecture; reference implementation not built. Generative AI tools assisted research synthesis, consolidation, language editing, consistency review, and publication preparation. The named author directed the work and is responsible for the released content and publication decision.
